core_model.structure_summary: A deterministic algebraic map that forms normalized similarity weights from inner products of two matrices (rescaled by a dimension factor and passed through a row-normalizing exponential) and applies them to a third matrix, with an outer concatenation of parallel such maps.

variables.meaning: output projection matrix and dimension scaling factor

deterministic_skeleton.summary: A normalized bilinear form: exponentiate and row-normalize a scaled matrix inner product, then use it to take a convex combination of rows of another matrix.

notes: This is a survey (resource-efficient LLMs) with no genuine central mathematical model of its own; the only display equations are the standard scaled-dot-product / multi-head attention map, which is representative background rather than a result the paper is about. Reported with low confidence for that reason. softmax gives both a normalization (simplex) constraint and a log/exp (entropy-like) structure.
